strengths: 15+ years of fullstack experience with sustained TypeScript/Node.js product work at Optilyz, RIDE Capital, and re:cinq
Hands-on AI agent development at re:cinq, including MCP code generators and agentic-workflow frameworks — a rare explicit match for the AI agent requirement
Deep testing culture: TDD/pairing/trunk-based development at RIDE, author of test tooling (Fluent-Asserts, Trial), and created testing courses/workshops
concerns: No Nest.js experience mentioned anywhere on the résumé, and Vitest/Jest/Playwright are not explicitly named despite the strong testing background
Kotlin and Python are absent; Java exposure is limited to robotics work at Altom (2016–2018), so the secondary Kotlin/Java stack is weakly covered
Frontend depth leans heavily on Ember.js; React appears only in the Optilyz role, so modern React proficiency is thinner than the backend profile

key_factors.factor: Missing Nest.js and limited React/Kotlin evidence

response.justification: The role's core competencies are end-to-end TypeScript/Node/React delivery, testing expertise, cloud/database fluency, and AI agent development. The candidate exceeds the 6+ year bar with roughly 15 years, shows direct evidence on TypeScript/Node/React/MongoDB/AWS CDK (Optilyz pricing platform), Kubernetes and Docker (GISCollective, Altom dev-ops), and explicit AI-agent/MCP tooling work at re:cinq. Testing competency is exceptionally documented (TDD at RIDE, authored test frameworks, taught testing courses), even though Vitest itself is not named. Gaps are confined to specific tools: Nest.js is absent, React evidence is limited to one role, and Kotlin/Python are missing with only dated Java exposure. Given the strong architecture, polyglot, and craftsmanship evidence, these framework-level gaps look learnable rather than disqualifying.
